column:orders.checkout_surface.description: Identifies the checkout interface or integration method used to create the order. Observed values include 'web' (browser-based checkout), 'mobile_sdk' (mobile app integration), 'api' (direct API call), 'plugin' (e-commerce plugin), and 'dashboard' (Razorpay dashboard). This column is useful for comparing order volumes, conversion rates, and payment success across different checkout surfaces.
column:orders.checkout_surface.name: checkout_surface
terms: identifies the checkout interface or integration method used to create the order. observed values include 'web' (browser based checkout), 'mobile sdk' (mobile app integration), 'api' (direct api call), 'plugin' (e commerce plugin), and 'dashboard' (razorpay dashboard). this column is useful for comparing order volumes, conversion rates, and payment success across different checkout surfaces.

column:orders.order_amount.description: The amount associated with the checkout order at the time it was created, expressed as a decimal value. This represents the intended transaction value before any payment attempt is made. It can be compared to the actual payment amount in the payments table to identify discrepancies or partial payments. Values range from small amounts to over one lakh rupees in this dataset.
column:orders.order_amount.name: order_amount

column:orders.order_ref.description: A unique reference code assigned to each checkout order created on the Razorpay platform. This identifier links the orders table to the payments table, allowing analysts to track how many payment attempts were made for a given order and whether the order was ultimately paid. Each order_ref appears once in the orders table and may appear multiple times in payments if the user retried payment.

metadata.sql_guidance: Join orders to payments on order_ref to count attempts per order.

column:orders.order_ref.name: order_ref
terms: a unique reference code assigned to each checkout order created on the razorpay platform. this identifier links the orders table to the payments table, allowing analysts to track how many payment attempts were made for a given order and whether the order was ultimately paid. each order ref appears once in the orders table and may appear multiple times in payments if the user retried payment.
join orders to payments on order ref to count attempts per order.

column:orders.order_state.description: Indicates the current state of the checkout order in its lifecycle. Possible values are: 'paid' (a successful payment was completed), 'created' (the order exists but no successful payment has been made yet), 'cancelled' (the order was cancelled before payment), and 'expired' (the order timed out without a successful payment). This column is essential for funnel analysis — for example, calculating the share of orders that convert to paid versus those that drop off.

metadata.sql_guidance: Filter order_state = 'paid' to count converted orders; use all states for funnel analysis.

column:orders.order_state.name: order_state
terms: filter order state = 'paid' to count converted orders; use all states for funnel analysis.

column:orders.order_time.description: The date and time at which the checkout order was created on the Razorpay platform. This timestamp is used for time-based analysis of order creation trends, such as daily or monthly order volumes, and for funnel analysis comparing when orders were created versus when payment attempts occurred.

column:orders.product_area.description: Categorises the order by the Razorpay product line it belongs to. Observed values are 'checkout', 'payments', 'payouts', 'subscriptions', and 'marketplace'. This column allows analysts to segment order and payment metrics by product area — for example, comparing TPV or conversion rates across subscriptions versus marketplace orders.

column:orders.user_ref.description: Identifies the Razorpay user or merchant account that initiated the checkout order. This reference links the orders table to the users table (for account-level attributes such as merchant type and plan) and to the user_attributes table (for demographic and risk attributes). It can also be used to join with the payments table to trace a user's full order and payment history.

column:payment_attributes.authentication.description: Describes how the payer was authenticated during the payment transaction. Observed values are 'upi_pin' (UPI PIN entry), 'otp_or_3ds' (one-time password or 3D Secure for cards), 'bank_auth' (bank-level authentication, typically for netbanking), and 'wallet_auth' (wallet-specific authentication). This column can be used to analyse how authentication method affects payment success rates.

column:payment_attributes.issuer_name.description: Identifies the bank or financial institution associated with the payment rail. Observed issuers include major Indian banks such as HDFC, ICICI, SBI, Axis, Kotak, RBL, and Yes Bank, as well as NPCI (the National Payments Corporation of India, which governs UPI). This column can be used to analyse payment success rates or volumes broken down by issuing bank.
column:payment_attributes.issuer_name.name: issuer_name

column:payment_attributes.rail_ref.description: A unique reference code that identifies a specific payment rail or routing configuration. This is the join key between the payment_attributes table and the payments table. When analysing payment metrics by rail type (UPI, credit card, NEFT, etc.), payments is joined to payment_attributes on this field to resolve the rail type and other routing attributes for each transaction.

column:payment_attributes.rail_type.description: Describes the payment method or network associated with a rail. Observed values are UPI, CC (credit card), DC (debit card), IMPS, NEFT, NETBANKING, and WALLET. This is the primary dimension for slicing payment metrics by payment method. For example, to calculate TPV or PSR by UPI versus credit card, join payments to payment_attributes on rail_ref and group by rail_type. UPI is the most common rail in this dataset, followed by credit cards and debit cards.

metadata.sql_guidance: Join payments to payment_attributes on rail_ref then group by rail_type to slice TPV or PSR by payment method.

column:payment_attributes.risk_band.description: Categorises the payment rail into a risk segment for transaction monitoring purposes. Observed values are 'low', 'medium', and 'high'. The majority of rails in this dataset are classified as low risk. This column can be used to filter or segment payment analysis by risk level, for example to understand the volume or success rate of high-risk payment routes.

column:payment_attributes.route_partner.description: Identifies the internal Razorpay routing partner or processing entity used for a given payment rail. Observed values are rzp_router_a through rzp_router_d, representing different Razorpay route or processing partners. column:payment_attributes.settlement_spe.description: Indicates the settlement timeline for funds processed through this payment rail. Observed values are 'instant' (funds settle immediately), 'same_day' (funds settle on the same business day), and 't_plus_1' (funds settle the next business day). This attribute is useful for analysing settlement patterns across different payment methods and for understanding liquidity implications by rail type.

column:payments.amount.description: The monetary value of the payment attempt. This field is the measure used to calculate TPV (total payment volume), which is defined as the sum of amounts where payment_status is SUCCESS. It should not be summed across all payment statuses unless the intent is to measure attempted volume rather than completed volume. Values in this dataset range from approximately 53 to nearly 148,000 rupees.

metadata.sql_guidance: Sum amount WHERE payment_status = 'SUCCESS' to calculate TPV; do not filter by status for attempted volume.

column:payments.amount.name: amount
terms: sum amount where payment status = 'success' to calculate tpv; do not filter by status for attempted volume.

column:payments.order_ref.description: Identifies the checkout order associated with this payment attempt. Multiple payment attempts can belong to the same order (for example, if a user retries after a failed attempt). This field links the payments table to the orders table, enabling order-to-payment funnel analysis and allowing analysts to see how many attempts were made per order.

column:payments.payment_ref.description: A unique reference code assigned to each individual payment attempt on the Razorpay platform. This is the grain of the payments table — each row represents one payment attempt. It is used to count total payment attempts (the denominator in PSR calculations) and to uniquely identify transactions for debugging or reconciliation purposes.

metadata.sql_guidance: Count distinct payment_ref for total payment attempts (PSR denominator).

column:payments.payment_status.description: Indicates the outcome or current state of the payment attempt. Observed values are SUCCESS (payment completed), FAILED (payment did not go through), AUTHORIZED (payment authorised but not yet captured), and REFUNDED (payment was reversed). This field is the filter used in nearly all business metrics: TPV and active user metrics require payment_status = 'SUCCESS'. PSR is calculated as the count of SUCCESS rows divided by the total count of all rows (all statuses). Do not pre-filter to SUCCESS when calculating PSR, as the denominator must include all attempts.

metadata.sql_guidance: PSR = COUNT(*) FILTER (payment_status='SUCCESS') / COUNT(*) across all statuses; do not pre-filter for PSR denominator.

column:payments.payment_time.description: The exact date and time at which the payment attempt was recorded. This is the primary time dimension for all payment analytics. It is used to calculate daily active users (DAU), monthly active users (MAU), calendar-month TPV and PSR, retained users (activity across a 3-month window), and churned users (no activity in the past 15 days). For calendar-month grouping, this field is truncated to the month. Data in this dataset spans from December 2025 to June 2026.

metadata.sql_guidance: Truncate to month for calendar-month grouping; use date part for DAU; data spans Dec 2025 to Jun 2026.

column:payments.rail_ref.description: Identifies the payment rail or routing configuration used for this specific payment attempt. column:payments.user_ref.description: Identifies the Razorpay user or merchant account that initiated the payment attempt. This field is central to all user-level metrics: active users (at least one successful payment in a calendar month), DAU (at least one successful payment on a given day), MAU (at least three successful payments in a calendar month), retained users (at least 10 successful transactions across 3 consecutive months with activity in every month), and churned users (no successful payment in the past 15 days). Join to users or user_attributes on this field to add demographic or account-level context.

metadata.sql_guidance: Filter payment_status = 'SUCCESS' before counting distinct user_ref for active user metrics.

dimension:payment_attributes.authenticat.description: Describes how the payer was authenticated during the payment transaction. Observed values are 'upi_pin' (UPI PIN entry), 'otp_or_3ds' (one-time password or 3D Secure for cards), 'bank_auth' (bank-level authentication, typically for netbanking), and 'wallet_auth' (wallet-specific authentication). This column can be used to analyse how authentication method affects payment success rates.
dimension:payment_attributes.authenticat.name: authentication_mode
terms: describes how the payer was authenticated during the payment transaction. observed values are 'upi pin' (upi pin entry), 'otp or 3ds' (one time password or 3d secure for cards), 'bank auth' (bank level authentication, typically for netbanking), and 'wallet auth' (wallet specific authentication). this column can be used to analyse how authentication method affects payment success rates.

dimension:payment_attributes.issuer_name.description: Identifies the bank or financial institution associated with the payment rail. Observed issuers include major Indian banks such as HDFC, ICICI, SBI, Axis, Kotak, RBL, and Yes Bank, as well as NPCI (the National Payments Corporation of India, which governs UPI). This column can be used to analyse payment success rates or volumes broken down by issuing bank.
dimension:payment_attributes.issuer_name.name: issuer_name
terms: identifies the bank or financial institution associated with the payment rail. observed issuers include major indian banks such as hdfc, icici, sbi, axis, kotak, rbl, and yes bank, as well as npci (the national payments corporation of india, which governs upi). this column can be used to analyse payment success rates or volumes broken down by issuing bank.

dimension:payment_attributes.rail_type.description: Describes the payment method or network associated with a rail. Observed values are UPI, CC (credit card), DC (debit card), IMPS, NEFT, NETBANKING, and WALLET. This is the primary dimension for slicing payment metrics by payment method. For example, to calculate TPV or PSR by UPI versus credit card, join payments to payment_attributes on rail_ref and group by rail_type. UPI is the most common rail in this dataset, followed by credit cards and debit cards.
dimension:payment_attributes.rail_type.name: rail_type
terms: describes the payment method or network associated with a rail. observed values are upi, cc (credit card), dc (debit card), imps, neft, netbanking, and wallet. this is the primary dimension for slicing payment metrics by payment method. for example, to calculate tpv or psr by upi versus credit card, join payments to payment attributes on rail ref and group by rail type. upi is the most common rail in this dataset, followed by credit cards and debit cards.
join payments to payment attributes on rail ref then group by rail type to slice tpv or psr by payment method.

dimension:payment_attributes.risk_band.description: Categorises the payment rail into a risk segment for transaction monitoring purposes. Observed values are 'low', 'medium', and 'high'. The majority of rails in this dataset are classified as low risk. This column can be used to filter or segment payment analysis by risk level, for example to understand the volume or success rate of high-risk payment routes.
dimension:payment_attributes.risk_band.name: risk_band
terms: categorises the payment rail into a risk segment for transaction monitoring purposes. observed values are 'low', 'medium', and 'high'. the majority of rails in this dataset are classified as low risk. this column can be used to filter or segment payment analysis by risk level, for example to understand the volume or success rate of high risk payment routes.

dimension:payment_attributes.route_partn.description: Identifies the internal Razorpay routing partner or processing entity used for a given payment rail. Observed values are rzp_router_a through rzp_router_d, representing different Razorpay route or processing partners. This column can be used to compare performance metrics such as success rates across different routing configurations.
dimension:payment_attributes.route_partn.name: route_partner
terms: identifies the internal razorpay routing partner or processing entity used for a given payment rail. observed values are rzp router a through rzp router d, representing different razorpay route or processing partners. this column can be used to compare performance metrics such as success rates across different routing configurations.

dimension:payment_attributes.settlement_.description: Indicates the settlement timeline for funds processed through this payment rail. Observed values are 'instant' (funds settle immediately), 'same_day' (funds settle on the same business day), and 't_plus_1' (funds settle the next business day). This attribute is useful for analysing settlement patterns across different payment methods and for understanding liquidity implications by rail type.
dimension:payment_attributes.settlement_.name: settlement_speed
terms: indicates the settlement timeline for funds processed through this payment rail. observed values are 'instant' (funds settle immediately), 'same day' (funds settle on the same business day), and 't plus 1' (funds settle the next business day). this attribute is useful for analysing settlement patterns across different payment methods and for understanding liquidity implications by rail type.

dimension:payments.payment_status.description: Indicates the outcome or current state of the payment attempt. Observed values are SUCCESS (payment completed), FAILED (payment did not go through), AUTHORIZED (payment authorised but not yet captured), and REFUNDED (payment was reversed). This field is the filter used in nearly all business metrics: TPV and active user metrics require payment_status = 'SUCCESS'. PSR is calculated as the count of SUCCESS rows divided by the total count of all rows (all statuses). Do not pre-filter to SUCCESS when calculating PSR, as the denominator must include all attempts.
dimension:payments.payment_status.name: payment_status
terms: indicates the outcome or current state of the payment attempt. observed values are success (payment completed), failed (payment did not go through), authorized (payment authorised but not yet captured), and refunded (payment was reversed). this field is the filter used in nearly all business metrics: tpv and active user metrics require payment status = 'success'. psr is calculated as the count of success rows divided by the total count of all rows (all statuses). do not pre filter to success when calculating psr, as the denominator must include all attempts.
psr = count(*) filter (payment status='success') / count(*) across all statuses; do not pre filter for psr denominator.

dimension:payments.payment_time.description: The exact date and time at which the payment attempt was recorded. This is the primary time dimension for all payment analytics. It is used to calculate daily active users (DAU), monthly active users (MAU), calendar-month TPV and PSR, retained users (activity across a 3-month window), and churned users (no activity in the past 15 days). For calendar-month grouping, this field is truncated to the month. Data in this dataset spans from December 2025 to June 2026.
dimension:payments.payment_time.name: payment_time
terms: the exact date and time at which the payment attempt was recorded. this is the primary time dimension for all payment analytics. it is used to calculate daily active users (dau), monthly active users (mau), calendar month tpv and psr, retained users (activity across a 3 month window), and churned users (no activity in the past 15 days). for calendar month grouping, this field is truncated to the month. data in this dataset spans from december 2025 to june 2026.
truncate to month for calendar month grouping; use date part for dau; data spans dec 2025 to jun 2026.

dimension:user_attributes.age.description: The age of the user or merchant account holder in years. Values in this dataset range from 19 to 68. This column can be used to segment payment metrics such as TPV, PSR, or active user counts by age group, for example to compare behaviour across younger versus older users.
dimension:user_attributes.age.name: age
terms: the age of the user or merchant account holder in years. values in this dataset range from 19 to 68. this column can be used to segment payment metrics such as tpv, psr, or active user counts by age group, for example to compare behaviour across younger versus older users.

dimension:user_attributes.city.description: The city associated with the user's account. Observed values include 21 Indian cities such as Mumbai, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Chennai, Pune, New Delhi, Ahmedabad, Surat, Jaipur, and others. This column enables geographic segmentation of payment metrics at the city level. It can be combined with the state column for broader regional analysis.
dimension:user_attributes.city.name: city
terms: the city associated with the user's account. observed values include 21 indian cities such as mumbai, bengaluru, hyderabad, chennai, pune, new delhi, ahmedabad, surat, jaipur, and others. this column enables geographic segmentation of payment metrics at the city level. it can be combined with the state column for broader regional analysis.

dimension:user_attributes.gender.description: The self-reported gender of the user or merchant account holder. Observed values are 'M' (male), 'F' (female), and 'Other'. The majority of users in this dataset identify as male. This column can be used to segment payment or order metrics by gender for demographic analysis.
dimension:user_attributes.gender.name: gender
terms: the self reported gender of the user or merchant account holder. observed values are 'm' (male), 'f' (female), and 'other'. the majority of users in this dataset identify as male. this column can be used to segment payment or order metrics by gender for demographic analysis.

dimension:user_attributes.kyc_status.description: Indicates the KYC (Know Your Customer) verification state for the user's account. Observed values are 'verified' (KYC completed successfully), 'pending' (KYC submitted but not yet reviewed), and 'rejected' (KYC was not approved). The large majority of users in this dataset are verified. This column is useful for compliance-related analysis and for understanding whether KYC status correlates with payment behaviour or success rates.
dimension:user_attributes.kyc_status.name: kyc_status
terms: indicates the kyc (know your customer) verification state for the user's account. observed values are 'verified' (kyc completed successfully), 'pending' (kyc submitted but not yet reviewed), and 'rejected' (kyc was not approved). the large majority of users in this dataset are verified. this column is useful for compliance related analysis and for understanding whether kyc status correlates with payment behaviour or success rates.

dimension:user_attributes.risk_band.description: Classifies the user into a risk tier for transaction monitoring and fraud management purposes. Observed values are 'low', 'medium', and 'high', with the majority of users classified as low risk. This column can be used to filter or segment payment analysis by user risk level, for example to compare payment success rates or volumes across risk tiers.
dimension:user_attributes.risk_band.name: risk_band
terms: classifies the user into a risk tier for transaction monitoring and fraud management purposes. observed values are 'low', 'medium', and 'high', with the majority of users classified as low risk. this column can be used to filter or segment payment analysis by user risk level, for example to compare payment success rates or volumes across risk tiers.

dimension:user_attributes.state.description: The Indian state associated with the user's account. Observed values are Gujarat, Telangana, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Rajasthan, Delhi, and Maharashtra. This column is the primary geographic dimension for state-level analysis of payment volumes, success rates, or user activity. It is explicitly noted in the business context as the field for geographic slicing.
dimension:user_attributes.state.name: state
terms: the indian state associated with the user's account. observed values are gujarat, telangana, karnataka, tamil nadu, rajasthan, delhi, and maharashtra. this column is the primary geographic dimension for state level analysis of payment volumes, success rates, or user activity. it is explicitly noted in the business context as the field for geographic slicing.
use state for geographic slicing; combine with city for finer granularity.

dimension:users.account_state.description: Indicates the operational state of the Razorpay merchant account. Observed values are 'active' (account is fully operational), 'paused' (account activity is temporarily suspended), 'restricted' (account has limitations applied), and 'closed' (account is no longer active). The vast majority of accounts in this dataset are active. This column is useful for filtering analyses to active accounts or for understanding the distribution of account health.
dimension:users.account_state.name: account_state
terms: filter account state = 'active' to restrict analysis to operational accounts.

indicates the operational state of the razorpay merchant account. observed values are 'active' (account is fully operational), 'paused' (account activity is temporarily suspended), 'restricted' (account has limitations applied), and 'closed' (account is no longer active). the vast majority of accounts in this dataset are active. this column is useful for filtering analyses to active accounts or for understanding the distribution of account health.

dimension:users.acquisition_channel.description: Indicates the channel through which the Razorpay merchant account was onboarded. Observed values are 'self_serve' (merchant signed up independently), 'sales' (acquired through a sales team), 'referral' (referred by another user), 'partner' (onboarded via a partner), and 'marketplace' (acquired through a marketplace integration). This column is useful for analysing the quality and payment behaviour of accounts by acquisition source.
dimension:users.acquisition_channel.name: acquisition_channel
terms: indicates the channel through which the razorpay merchant account was onboarded. observed values are 'self serve' (merchant signed up independently), 'sales' (acquired through a sales team), 'referral' (referred by another user), 'partner' (onboarded via a partner), and 'marketplace' (acquired through a marketplace integration). this column is useful for analysing the quality and payment behaviour of accounts by acquisition source.

dimension:users.country.description: The country associated with the Razorpay merchant account. All 1,000 accounts in this dataset have a value of 'India', consistent with Razorpay's primary market. This column may be used for filtering in multi-country contexts, but currently provides no segmentation value within this dataset.
dimension:users.country.name: country
terms: the country associated with the razorpay merchant account. all 1,000 accounts in this dataset have a value of 'india', consistent with razorpay's primary market. this column may be used for filtering in multi country contexts, but currently provides no segmentation value within this dataset.

dimension:users.merchant_type.description: Classifies the Razorpay merchant account by business size or type. Observed values are 'enterprise', 'mid_market', 'smb' (small and medium business), 'startup', and 'platform'. This column is used to segment payment volumes, success rates, or user activity by merchant tier, for example to compare TPV between enterprise and SMB merchants.
dimension:users.merchant_type.name: merchant_type
terms: classifies the razorpay merchant account by business size or type. observed values are 'enterprise', 'mid market', 'smb' (small and medium business), 'startup', and 'platform'. this column is used to segment payment volumes, success rates, or user activity by merchant tier, for example to compare tpv between enterprise and smb merchants.

dimension:users.platform_plan.description: Identifies the Razorpay platform plan or pricing tier associated with the merchant account. Observed values are 'starter', 'growth', 'scale', and 'enterprise'. This column can be used to segment payment metrics by plan tier, for example to compare average TPV or payment success rates across starter versus enterprise plan merchants.
dimension:users.platform_plan.name: platform_plan
terms: identifies the razorpay platform plan or pricing tier associated with the merchant account. observed values are 'starter', 'growth', 'scale', and 'enterprise'. this column can be used to segment payment metrics by plan tier, for example to compare average tpv or payment success rates across starter versus enterprise plan merchants.

dimension:users.signup_time.description: The date and time at which the Razorpay merchant or user account was registered. This field can be used to calculate account tenure, cohort users by signup month, or analyse how payment behaviour evolves over time since onboarding. Data in this dataset spans signups from December 2024 through May 2026.

pattern:history:orders_payment_attribute.description: orders + payment_attributes + payments + user_attributes + users pattern using orders.checkout_surface, orders.order_ref, payment_attributes.authentication_mode, payment_attributes.rail_ref

metadata.sql_template: SELECT o.checkout_surface, pa.authentication_mode, sum(CASE WHEN p.payment_status = {{string}} THEN p.amount ELSE {{number}} END) AS tpv, sum(CASE WHEN p.payment_status = {{string}} THEN {{number}} ELSE {{number}} END) AS successful_payments, count(*) AS total_payments, sum(CASE WHEN p.payment_status = {{string}} THEN {{number}} ELSE {{number}} END)::DOUBLE / nullif(count(*), {{number}}) AS psr FROM payments p JOIN orders o ON p.order_ref = o.order_ref JOIN user_attributes ua ON p.user_ref = ua.user_ref JOIN users u ON p.user_ref = u.user_ref JOIN payment_attributes pa ON p.rail_ref = pa.rail_ref WHERE p.payment_time >= {{timestamp}} AND p.payment_time < {{timestamp}} AND ua.risk_band = {{string}} AND ua.state = {{string}} AND ua.kyc_status = {{string}} AND u.account_state = {{string}} GROUP BY o.checkout_surface, pa.authentication_mode HAVING count(*) >= {{number}} ORDER BY tpv DESC

pattern:history:payments_user_attributes.description: payments + user_attributes pattern using payments.payment_status, payments.payment_time, payments.user_ref, user_attributes.risk_band

metadata.sql_template: WITH prior_users AS ( SELECT DISTINCT p.user_ref FROM payments p WHERE p.payment_status = {{string}} AND p.payment_time < {{timestamp}} - INTERVAL {{number}} DAY ), recent_users AS ( SELECT DISTINCT p.user_ref FROM payments p WHERE p.payment_status = {{string}} AND p.payment_time >= {{timestamp}} - INTERVAL {{number}} DAY ) SELECT ua.risk_band, count(*) AS churned_users FROM prior_users pu JOIN user_attributes ua ON pu.user_ref = ua.user_ref LEFT JOIN recent_users ru ON pu.user_ref = ru.user_ref WHERE ru.user_ref IS NULL AND ua.risk_band = {{string}} GROUP BY ua.risk_band

table:orders.description: The orders table records every checkout order initiated on the Razorpay platform before a payment attempt occurs. Each row represents a single order with its value, lifecycle state, the surface through which checkout happened, and the Razorpay product area it belongs to. This table is the starting point for order-to-payment funnel analysis — for example, understanding how many orders were created versus paid, cancelled, or expired. It links to the payments table via order_ref, allowing analysts to track how many payment attempts were made against each order.
table:orders.name: orders
terms: the orders table records every checkout order initiated on the razorpay platform before a payment attempt occurs. each row represents a single order with its value, lifecycle state, the surface through which checkout happened, and the razorpay product area it belongs to. this table is the starting point for order to payment funnel analysis — for example, understanding how many orders were created versus paid, cancelled, or expired. it links to the payments table via order ref, allowing analysts to track how many payment attempts were made against each order.

table:payment_attributes.description: The payment_attributes table describes the characteristics of each payment rail or route used on the Razorpay platform. Each row represents a unique payment rail identified by rail_ref, and captures the rail type (UPI, credit card, debit card, NEFT, IMPS, netbanking, or wallet), the issuing bank, the Razorpay routing partner, how quickly funds settle, the authentication method used, and the risk classification of the rail. This table is joined to the payments table on rail_ref to slice payment metrics such as TPV (total payment volume) and PSR (payment success rate) by payment method or network. It is the primary source for any analysis that needs to break down performance by UPI versus cards versus NEFT or other rails.
table:payment_attributes.name: payment_attributes
terms: the payment attributes table describes the characteristics of each payment rail or route used on the razorpay platform. each row represents a unique payment rail identified by rail ref, and captures the rail type (upi, credit card, debit card, neft, imps, netbanking, or wallet), the issuing bank, the razorpay routing partner, how quickly funds settle, the authentication method used, and the risk classification of the rail. this table is joined to the payments table on rail ref to slice payment metrics such as tpv (total payment volume) and psr (payment success rate) by payment method or network. it is the primary source for any analysis that needs to break down performance by upi versus cards versus neft or other rails.

table:payments.description: The payments table is the central fact table for Razorpay payment analytics. Each row represents a single payment attempt, capturing the transaction amount, the lifecycle status (SUCCESS, FAILED, AUTHORIZED, or REFUNDED), the payment rail used, the order it belongs to, the user or merchant who made the attempt, and the exact timestamp. This table is used to calculate all key business metrics: TPV (total payment volume, summing amounts where status is SUCCESS), PSR (payment success rate, successful attempts divided by all attempts), DAU (distinct users with at least one successful payment on a given day), MAU (users with at least three successful payments in a calendar month), retained users (at least 10 successful transactions across 3 consecutive months), and churned users (no successful payment in the past 15 days). Payment time is used for all calendar-period grouping. Join to payment_attributes on rail_ref to slice by payment method.
table:payments.name: payments

table:user_attributes.description: The user_attributes table stores descriptive profile information about each Razorpay user or merchant account. Each row corresponds to one user and includes their age, gender, city, Indian state, risk segment, and KYC verification status. This table is used to slice payment metrics — such as TPV, PSR, active users, or churned users — by demographic or geographic dimensions. For example, analysts can compare payment success rates across states like Gujarat, Karnataka, or Maharashtra, or filter by KYC status to understand verified versus pending users. The risk_band column supports transaction monitoring and fraud-related segmentation. Join to payments or orders on user_ref to combine behavioral data with demographic attributes.

table:users.description: The users table contains one row per Razorpay merchant or user account and captures account-level attributes set at or after signup. It records when the account was created, the type of merchant (enterprise, mid-market, SMB, startup, or platform), how the account was acquired (self-serve, sales, referral, partner, or marketplace), the platform pricing plan, the current account state (active, paused, restricted, or closed), and the country (all accounts in this dataset are India-based). This table is used to segment payment and order activity by merchant tier, acquisition source, or account health. Join to payments or orders on user_ref to combine transactional metrics with account-level dimensions.

value:orders.order_state:cancelled.name: cancelled

value:payments.payment_status:success.name: SUCCESS
terms: indicates the outcome or current state of the payment attempt. observed values are success (payment completed), failed (payment did not go through), authorized (payment authorised but not yet captured), and refunded (payment was reversed). this field is the filter used in nearly all business metrics: tpv and active user metrics require payment status = 'success'. psr is calculated as the count of success rows divided by the total count of all rows (all statuses). do not pre filter to success when calculating psr, as the denominator must include all attempts.
